title: silene × dubia
intro: Silene × dubia, also known as Bladder Campion, is a hybrid plant species within the Caryophyllaceae family, resulting from the cross between Silene inflata and Silene viscosa. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits associated with Silene × dubia include its potential to alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Silene × dubia is often prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and can also be consumed as a capsule or used topically in the form of a cream or ointment.
